title: crossopetalum puberulum
intro: Crossopetalum puberulum, also known as the West Indian cherry or yellow guava, is a plant species in the Celastraceae family that is native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Crossopetalum puberulum include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antihemorrhagic agent. Herbal preparations of Crossopetalum puberulum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
